item.title: Hero pilot recounts crash landing in Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Ian Nixon, a veteran pilot from the Bahamas, put the plane he was flying down in the ocean without anyone suffering serious injuries.

item.title: U.S. moving to indict Cuba's Raúl Castro, sources say
item.description: The potential indictment — which must be approved by a grand jury — is expected to focus on Cuba's 1996 downing of two planes operated by a humanitarian group.

item.title: American passenger from hantavirus-hit cruise ship on life in quarantine
item.description: The number of people being monitored for hantavirus in the United States has grown to 41, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said Thursday.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Douglas Edwards' last broadcast
item.description: When Douglas Edwards began presenting the CBS Television News in 1947, he made history as the first anchor of an American TV news show. Although he left the role in 1962, Edwards remained at CBS, doing considerable TV and radio work, until 1988. Major Garrett introduces Edwards' final radio broadcast.

item.title: How redistricting efforts could affect midterms, 2028 election
item.description: South Carolina's Republican Gov. Henry McMaster has called for a special legislative session centered on redrawing the state's congressional map ahead of the November midterm elections. The legislature is expected to pass a map that could impact the state's only Democratic district. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more on how redistricting efforts across the country could impact future elections.

item.title: Supreme Court allows freight brokers to face lawsuits in some truck crashes
item.description: The Supreme Court handed down a unanimous ruling on Thursday that will allow logistics companies or freight brokers to be sued for liability in some truck crashes. CBS News senior national correspondent Jim Axelrod breaks down the case.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

item.title: Chinese Americans discuss hopes for Trump-Xi summit
item.description: CBS News' Anna Schecter went to Flushing, Queens, to ask Chinese Americans for their thoughts on the Trump-Xi summit. Schecter joins "The Takeout" to relay what she heard.

item.title: Bipartisan bill aims to help civilians exposed to toxic burn pits
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers unveiled a bill to help civilians, including law enforcement agents, receive workers' compensation for illnesses like cancer that are often associated with toxic exposure to burn pits.

item.title: What did Trump and Xi discuss about Taiwan, Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: President Trump wrapped up his first of two expected meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing Thursday. The two sat down in a closed-door session for over two hours. Anna Coren has more on the meeting.

item.title: Cuba considers U.S. offer of $100 million in aid amid power grid collapse
item.description: Cuba's national energy grid has suffered a major failure, cutting power to the island's eastern provinces.

item.title: Rep. Lou Correa says "we owe it to our veterans" to research psychedelic drug therapies
item.description: In April, President Trump signed an executive order that speeds up research and increases funding for psychedelic drug therapies. Democratic Rep. Lou Correa of California, who is leading a bipartisan effort to advance these therapies, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Jake Rosmarin, passenger from hantavirus-stricken cruise ship, on quarantine experience
item.description: Jake Rosmarin, a travel influencer who was on the M/V Hondius as it suffered a hantavirus outbreak, is one of 16 Americans quarantining at the University of Nebraska Medical Center. Rosmarin spoke with CBS News about how a five-week trip is now stretching into 12 weeks away from home.

item.title: Sen. Elissa Slotkin reacts to latest Iran news, Xi's warning to Trump on Taiwan
item.description: Taiwan, the Iran war and trade loomed over President Trump's meeting with China's Xi Jinping on Thursday. Democratic Sen. Elissa Slotkin of Michigan joins "The Takeout" to weigh in. Then, Lanhee Chen, fellow at the Hoover Institution, joins with analysis.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
item.description: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.

item.title: South Carolina governor calls special session on redistricting
item.description: As Republicans seek to retain control of the U.S. House, leaders of both parties nationwide have sought to redraw their congressional maps to net more seats for their parties.

item.title: A Chinese community in New York City reacts to the Trump-Xi Beijing summit
item.description: There has been palpable excitement about President Trump's state visit to China in the Queens neighborhood of Flushing, home to one of the largest Asian populations in the U.S.

item.title: Seized ship taken toward Iran as Trump and Xi agree on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A ship was taken by unknown parties toward Iranian waters after an Indian-flagged vessel was attacked off Oman.

item.title: Xi draws red line on Taiwan at Trump summit
item.description: During their bilateral meeting on Thursday, Chinese President Xi Jinping warned President Trump of "conflicts" if Taiwan is not "handled properly." CBS News foreign correspondent Anna Coren has the latest from Beijing.

item.title: How the Trump-Xi summit could impact the U.S.-China AI race
item.description: President Trump's visit to Beijing comes as the U.S. and China compete for artificial intelligence supremacy. Matt Sheehan, senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, joins with analysis.

item.title: Looking back at Scoop the service dog's appearances on CBS News 24/7
item.description: CBS News 24/7 is saying goodbye to Scoop, a service dog in training for Canine Companions, as she heads to puppy college.

item.title: Supreme Court rules trucking broker can be sued after deadly crashes
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that a trucking broker at the center of a CBS News investigation can be sued following deadly crashes. CBS News chief investigative correspondent Jim Axelrod has the details.

item.title: What was said in OpenAI lawsuit closing arguments
item.description: Lawyers presented closing arguments Thursday in the OpenAI trial pitting Elon Musk against its CEO, Sam Altman. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: FBI offers $200K for info on Air Force officer accused of spying for Iran
item.description: The FBI said Thursday that it's still trying to locate Monica Witt, who is accused of defecting to Iran in 2013 and revealing highly classified U.S. intelligence.

item.title: Video shows massive Russian aerial attack against Ukraine
item.description: Russia unleashed a massive two-day aerial barrage on Ukraine, the largest since the war began, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Thursday. These videos show the damage in the capital city, Kyiv.

item.title: Will Trump ask Putin to bring these Americans home?
item.description: Anastasia Antonov believes that her father, Aleksandr, was arrested by the Russian government last year because he is an American citizen. Now, she is appealing to President Trump to push Vladimir Putin's government to free her father.

item.title: House vote on Iran war powers resolution fails for third time
item.description: The vote on a Democratic resolution to rein in Mr. Trump's authority to strike Iran was 212-212, falling just short of a majority.

item.title: Rex Reed: A bold-faced-name life
item.description: The film critic and journalist talks about a life filled with movies, celebrities, and a penchant for speaking his mind

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will sue The New York Times
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government plans to file a defamation lawsuit against The New York Times.

item.title: U.S. delegation visited Cuba last week as Trump heaped pressure on island
item.description: A delegation of senior State Department representatives traveled to Cuba via a U.S. government plane last week, officials said, a diplomatic opening amid intense pressure from the Trump administration.
